ERRATA/SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF DEAN M. SCHMIDT IN OPPOS TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF SANCHEZ, THOMAS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KIE 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, RKI 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, BJI 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, BJI 1740 IRR INVESTORS, LLC, LIG 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, LIG 1740 IRR INVESTORS, LLC, AND DEFENDANT LEO EPP COMPANY; & DEFENDANT KIE 1740 IRR INVESTORS, LLC AND DEFENDANT RKI 1740 IRR INVESTORS, LLC (BOTH ADDED PER ERRATA NOTICE FILED ON 11/20/09), MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED AS TO "SUBSEQUENT OWNER DEFENDANTS." TRIBLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED REGARDING WHEN THEY BECAME OWNERS OF THE PROPERTY. TENANTS IN COMMON CONTRACT SIGNED 8/29/07 INDICATES THE ENTITIES HAD AN OWNERSHIP INTEREST BEFORE 10/18/07. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER THE OWNERSHIP INTEREST OCCURRED ON 8/29/07 OR LONG BEFORE THAT TIME. CC SEC. 1466 NOT APPLICABLE. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN ON THE OTHER ISSUES UNDER ANDREWS VS. FOSTER WHEELER (2006) 138 CAL.APP. 4TH 96 RELYING ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES. SUMMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO CONCEALMENT, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, AND PUNITIVE CLAIMS. NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANTS HAD ACTUAL KNOWLEDGE OF THE MOLD HAZARD. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED AS TO WRONGFUL EVICTION AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WHETHER DEFENDANTS SHOULD HAVE KNOWN OF MOLD HAZARD, AND WHETHER DEFENDANTS NEGLIGENTLY MISREPRESENTED THE CONDITION OF THE UNIT. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER LANDLORD DILIGENTLY INSPECTED THE PREMISES FOR DEFECTS AND PROPERLY ACTED TO CORRRECT THOSE DEFECTS. COURT RULED ON DEFENDANTS EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS, ORDER SIGNED, COPIES GIVEN TO COUNSEL. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR DEC-24-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS KIE 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, RKI 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, BJI 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, BJI 1740 IRR INVESTORS, LLC, LIG 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, LIG 1740 IRR INVESTORS, LLC, AND DEFENDANT LEO EPP COMPANY; & DEFENDANT KIE 1740 IRR INVESTORS, LLC AND DEFENDANT RKI 1740 IRR INVESTORS, LLC (BOTH ADDED PER ERRATA NOTICE FILED ON 11/20/09), MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Dean M. Schmidt, Esq. 296-1160 for pltf & opposing party; John C. Hentschel, Esq. of Jackson & Wallace LLP 982-6300 x3329 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for summary judgment denied as to "subsequent owner defendants." Trible issue of fact raised regarding when they became owners of the property. Tenants in common contract signed 8/29/07 indicates the entities had an ownership interest before 10/18/07. Triable issue of fact whether the ownership interest occurred on 8/29/07 or long before that time. CC sec. 1466 not applicable. Defendant shifted the burden on the other issues under Andrews vs. Foster Wheeler (2006) 138 Cal.App. 4th 96 relying on plaintiff's factually devoid special interrogatory responses. Summmary adjudication granted as to concealment, intentional infliction of emotional distress, and punitive claims. No evidence that defendants had actual knowledge of the mold hazard. Summary adjudication denied as to wrongful eviction and negligent misrepresentation. Triable issues of fact whether defendants should have known of mold hazard, and whether defendants negligently misrepresented the condition of the unit. Triable issue of fact whether landlord diligently inspected the premises for defects and properly acted to corrrect those defects. Court ruled on defendants evidentiary objections, order signed, copies given to counsel. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
